assertions.expectation: Strongly advises AGAINST moving to 'No channel' — it provides LESS control, not more
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Moving all clusters to "No channel" isn't generally recommended' and explains it provides less control through increased manual burden

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' only supports the basic 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion, while release channels support all three exclusion types including 'no minor or node upgrades' (up to EoS)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the specific exclusion types or the 30-day limitation of 'No channel' vs the more granular exclusion options available on release channels

assertions.expectation: Recommends staying on Regular channel (or moving to Stable/Extended) and using maintenance exclusions for control
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Stay on Regular channel but add maintenance windows and exclusion periods' and mentions Stable channel as an option

assertions.expectation: Specifically recommends the 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion as the way to prevent disruptive changes while allowing security patches
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the specific 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion type or explain how it allows security patches while preventing disruptive changes

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response does not mention Extended support or the 24-month support duration available only on release channels

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration and the industry is moving away from it
assertions.reason: Response does not characterize 'No channel' as legacy or mention industry trends away from this configuration

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maintenance windows control WHEN upgrades happen, while exclusions control WHAT upgrades happen — together they give fine-grained control
assertions.reason: Response mentions both maintenance windows and exclusions but does not clearly explain the distinction between controlling WHEN vs WHAT upgrades happen

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risks of 'No channel': no granular exclusions, no Extended support, risk of snowflaking clusters
assertions.reason: Response warns about general risks like security burden and operational complexity but does not specifically mention lack of granular exclusions, no Extended support, or cluster snowflaking risks
